title: onobrychis argentea subsp. cristata
intro: Onobrychis argentea subsp. cristata, also known as silver sainfoin or golden sainfoin, is a plant species within the legume family, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and yellow flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Onobrychis argentea subsp. cristata are attributed to its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as a natural antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to leverage its medicinal properties.
